name: Hu
description: Hu is the deification of the first word, the word of command, uttered by Atum (or Ra) at the moment of creation. He represents the authority of divine speech and the power inherent in the spoken word to manifest reality. Hu is often associated with Sia, the deification of perception and knowledge, forming a complementary pair that embodies the intelligence and authority necessary for the creation and maintenance of the cosmos. He is considered a vital component of the divine entourage that accompanies Ra on his solar barque, ensuring the sun god's commands are carried out and his journey through the underworld is successful. Hu's power is intrinsically linked to the concept of Ma'at, the cosmic order and truth, as his words uphold and reinforce its principles. Without Hu's authoritative utterance, the universe would lack structure and coherence. While not as prominently featured as some other Egyptian deities, Hu's role is crucial in understanding the Egyptian concept of creation and the power of divine language. He embodies the active force of the divine will, shaping and sustaining the world through the power of his spoken word. He is sometimes depicted as a man with a scepter, symbolizing his authority, or as part of Ra's entourage, emphasizing his role in the sun god's daily journey.
subtitle: The Divine Word of Creation.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the Universe
keyMyths.description: Hu is the first word spoken by Atum, bringing the universe into existence. This word is the foundation upon which all of creation is built.

keyMyths.title: Ra's Solar Journey
keyMyths.description: Hu accompanies Ra on his daily journey, ensuring Ra's commands are followed. He protects Ra from the dangers of the underworld with his powerful voice.

keyMyths.title: Upholding Ma'at
keyMyths.description: Hu's words reinforce Ma'at, the cosmic order and truth. He uses his power to maintain the balance of the universe and prevent chaos.
